why: Creates the district join spine needed to replace blunt state-average school costing.
caveat: Needs district boundary or geography crosswalk to connect PUMA-based ACS analysis to district-side admin data.

dataset: NCES F-33 School District Finance Survey
why: Moves school burden from state averages to district revenues, expenditures, and per-pupil spending.
caveat: Does not by itself identify English learner intensity or migrant-student counts.

why: Adds English learner, migrant-student, and related composition that likely drive marginal school costs.
caveat: Field availability varies by file specification and year.

why: Improves child concentration and poverty context for district burden sizing, especially for ages 5-17.
caveat: Crosswalk to NCES district IDs may need cleanup.

why: Separates cost burden from overcrowding and lets housing stress be measured rather than proxied by rent level alone.
caveat: Multi-year ACS-based product with lag; not person-level.

why: Improves subcounty rent surface where ACS median gross rent is noisy or unavailable for the right geography.
caveat: Administrative rent standard, not observed market-clearing rent.

why: Adds doubling-up, crowding, tenure, unit quality, and mobility that current ACS rent exposure does not capture well.
caveat: Metro coverage only; weaker for national small-area joins.

why: Enables actual commuting-flow exposure instead of inferring congestion from mean commute time alone.
caveat: Covered employment only; no direct immigrant-origin flag, so must be used as destination calibration.

why: Distinguishes local cost level from housing price appreciation and stops us from overloading a single rent statistic.
caveat: Composite fusion rather than one native table; county and metro coverage differ by product.

why: Lets us observe relocation responses directly instead of assuming natives stay put as local pressures change.
caveat: Tax-return universe misses some low-income and non-filing households.

dataset: Survey of Income and Program Participation
why: Best public-use route to a first serious tax/transfer microsimulation beyond ACS proxies.
caveat: Weaker local geography than ACS; should be used for calibration rather than direct local assignment.

dataset: ORR refugee arrivals + Annual Survey of Refugees
why: Separates refugee-like origin effects from labor-migrant origin effects and reduces origin-only confounding.
caveat: Covers refugee and related humanitarian streams, not all immigrant classes.
